evals.prompt: Execute the spec at specs/20260401-api-migration/. It has 4 subtasks across 3 phases. The spec status is Ready for Review.
evals.expected_output: Phased execution of all subtasks with adversarial verification after each, followed by final verification (tests, linter, quality audit) and an execution report written to the spec directory.
assertions: The spec index is loaded and the Subtask Manifest is validated before execution begins
User confirmation is requested before execution starts
Subtasks are executed in phase order, not out of order
Each completed subtask receives adversarial verification from a zoto-spec-judge subagent
The index manifest Status column is updated after each subtask verification
An execution report is written as execution-report-[feature-name]-[yyyymmdd].md
The execution report includes Subtask Results, Verification Results, and Files Modified sections
The spec index status is updated to Completed after user approval

evals.prompt: Resume executing specs/20260403-dark-mode/ — it was interrupted after subtask 02 completed. Subtasks 01 and 02 are Done, 03-05 are pending.
evals.expected_output: Execution resumes from subtask 03 without re-executing completed subtasks. Completed subtask deliverables are verified to still hold before continuing.
assertions: Completed subtasks (01 and 02) are not re-executed
Existing deliverables from completed subtasks are verified to still exist
Execution resumes from the next incomplete subtask based on dependency order
Adversarial verification is performed on newly completed subtasks
The final execution report reflects the full execution including resumed work

evals.prompt: Run /z-spec-execute on specs/20260402-test-infra/. This spec has 6 subtasks, phase 1 has 4 parallel subtasks, and the config sets parallelLimit to 2.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 subtasks are batched into groups of 2 (respecting parallelLimit), not all 4 at once. Each batch completes before the next starts.
assertions: The parallelLimit from config is read and respected
Phase 1 subtasks are batched into groups no larger than the parallelLimit
Each batch within a phase completes before the next batch starts
Dependencies are never violated — no subtask starts before its dependencies complete
Failed subtasks prevent dependent subtasks from starting
The execution report accurately reflects batching and parallel execution

evals.name: no-restart-budget-prompt
evals.prompt: Spawn one subtask via the spec-system spawn-prefix builder against a fixture .zoto/spec-system/config.yml that sets subagents.subtask.tokenBudget to 100000. Print the spawn prompt prefix. Then rewrite the same config.yml so subagents.subtask.tokenBudget is 250000 (without restarting the executor process). Re-run loadConfig with the previous mtime and print the second spawn prompt prefix. Show both prefixes verbatim.
evals.expected_output: The first spawn prefix line begins with `Token budget: 100000.` and the second spawn prefix line begins with `Token budget: 250000.` — proving the live config reload propagated to the next spawn without a process restart.
assertions: regex: Token budget: 100000\.
regex: Token budget: 250000\.
contains: Status files (you own these):
contains: Final state must be one of: completed | blocked | failed.

evals.name: aggregator-surfaces-blocker
evals.prompt: Run `pnpm --filter @zoto-agents/zoto-spec-system exec spec-aggregator --once --spec-dir <fixture-spec-dir>` against a fixture spec directory containing three subtask `.status.yml` sources all in `state: in_progress`. Capture the spec-root `status.yml` output. Then mutate one subtask's `.status.yml` to `state: blocked` with a `severity: error` `errors[]` entry whose message reads `Database fixture refused to seed`. Re-run the aggregator with `--once`. Show the resulting spec-root `status.yml` blockers section.
evals.expected_output: After the second `--once` run the spec-root `status.yml` `blockers[]` array contains one entry whose `subtask_id` matches the mutated subtask, whose `reason` matches the new error message, and whose `path` ends with `.status.md`. The first run's `blockers[]` was empty.
assertions: The spec-root status.yml snippet includes a YAML mapping key `blockers:` that introduces the blockers list, not merely the word blockers in free text.
regex: subtask_id:\s*['"]?\d{2}['"]?
contains: Database fixture refused to seed
regex: path:\s*['"]?[^'"\n]+\.status\.md['"]?

evals.prompt: The payments initiative index at `specs/acme-payments/spec-acme-payments-20260116.md` is marked Ready for Review. Follow the zoto-execute-spec flow: load configuration, validate the Subtask Manifest against each subtask file and dependency order, then show the execution summary table before requesting approval to spawn work.
assertions: The agent reads `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` from the repository root before validating paths under the configured specs directory.

The agent parses the Subtask Manifest as authoritative for subagent assignment, file paths, dependencies, and phase ordering, and flags missing files, mismatched Metadata, or a subtask depending on a higher-numbered ID before proposing execution.
The agent renders an Execution Summary whose Markdown includes the Subtask Manifest table shaped like the skill template and lists Parallel limit using `spec.parallelLimit` (default 4 when unset).
For the pre-execution gate the agent calls `askQuestion` titled Spec Execution Confirmation with yes or no options and does not replace that gate with a plain conversational yes-or-no prompt.

If `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` is absent when read, the agent stops and tells the operator to run `/z-spec-init` first to scaffold it, quoting the skill’s prescribed wording, before any execution summary.

When the configuration maps to defaults (including a comment-only file that parses as an empty mapping), the agent still uses the documented defaults for `specsDir`, `unitOfWork`, and parallel limit in user-facing limits.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent reports manifest validation results, prints an Execution Summary with subtask count, phase count, and parallel limit from configuration, and pauses on a structured confirmation rather than informal chat consent.

evals.prompt: Continue from the validated Acme payments spec: I already saw a clean manifest. I am approving launch—proceed with execution under the execute-spec skill.
assertions: Immediately after the Yes — begin execution choice resolves, the agent runs `date -u '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC'` and retains that value for the execution report’s Started field.
The agent does not spawn subagents for paid work until that confirmation and start-instant step ordering is satisfied.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: After structured approval, the agent captures a UTC start timestamp, keeps it for later duration math, and moves into phased subagent execution planning.
follow_ups: I selected Yes — begin execution in the Spec Execution Confirmation dialog.

evals.prompt: Run execute-spec against `specs/acme-mismatch/`—I need a hard pre-flight check before any subagents start.
assertions: The agent compares the manifest Subagent column to the subtask Metadata and stops with a concrete inconsistency report instead of calling `askQuestion` for Spec Execution Confirmation.
No execution-phase subagents are spawned while the manifest remains inconsistent.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.
fixture_justifications: Supplies a minimal valid spec-system config so paths resolve under `specs/` without depending on ambient workspace state.
Provides a spec index with a Subtask Manifest row for a deliberate Metadata versus manifest mismatch.
Hosts the referenced subtask file whose Metadata subagent conflicts with the manifest row so Step 1 must halt.

files.content:
specsDir: specs
spec:
  parallelLimit: 2
unitOfWork: spec

# Acme mismatch

## Subtask Manifest

| ID | File | Subagent | Dependencies | Phase |
|----|------|----------|-------------|-------|
| 01 | subtask-01-acme-mismatch-20260116.md | generalPurpose | — | 1 |

Status: Ready for Review

evals.expected_output: The agent reports the inconsistency, refuses to present an execution confirmation ask, and does not spawn subagents.

evals.prompt: `specs/risk-core/spec-risk-core-20260116.md` is still Draft, but I am explicitly authorizing execution now—use the execute-spec skill and treat that override as sufficient.
assertions: The agent follows Step 1 validation even when the index status is Draft because the user explicitly requested execution of a Draft spec per the When to Use guidance.
The agent still reaches the same `askQuestion`-based confirmation gate rather than bypassing user approval.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent acknowledges Draft status, still runs full manifest validation, and proceeds to the normal structured gate because the user explicitly authorized running a Draft.

evals.prompt: Execution is approved for the Acme payments spec. For Step 3, walk through how you will batch phase-1 subtasks when `spec.parallelLimit` is 3 and why manifest Subagent types are never swapped.
assertions: The agent enforces `spec.parallelLimit` as the maximum concurrent subtasks within a phase and describes batching when a phase has more rows than that limit.
The agent treats the manifest Subagent column as the only source of subagent type and states that types are not upgraded or substituted during execution.

The agent instructs each spawned worker with mandatory `TodoWrite` coverage for Deliverables Checklist items (ids like D01) and Definition of Done items (ids like DoD01) with the first actionable item marked in progress.
The agent directs workers to tick subtask Markdown checklist boxes in place as work completes and to record blockers in Execution Notes.
During parallel subtask work the agent reserves the full project test suite for the final verification phase and only allows targeted tests per subtask earlier.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent explains per-phase ordering, parallelism capped by config, batching when a phase exceeds the limit, and strict use of manifest-listed subagent types.

evals.prompt: For the modern Acme layout whose spec folder already contains `status/`, explain how you delegate work: executor versus subagent writes, heartbeat updates, and what the spawn prefix must say about token budget.
assertions: The agent states it does not write a subtask’s `.status.yml` after spawning; ownership and heartbeat updates belong to the spawned subagent while the aggregator reads those files.
The agent requires checklist changes through `.status.yml` (with `spec-status-roundtrip` or the heartbeat subcommand) and tells workers not to hand-edit `.status.md` during execution.

When `spec-spawn-prefix.ts` is used, the agent instructs that the prefix’s first line is exactly the Token budget line with the resolved integer N and that workers record `token_budget` in status accordingly.
The agent states that live-reloadable limits such as `subagents.*.tokenBudget` apply on the next spawn without restarting the whole executor.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent distinguishes executor behavior from subagent-owned status pairs, describes heartbeat and checklist updates via YAML with round-trip rendering, and references the token-budget first line rule.

evals.prompt: Once the Acme spec’s `status/` tree exists, how do you run aggregation and what skips rewrite churn on the spec-root status files?
assertions: The agent states that `spec-aggregator --watch` is backgrounded for the spec directory and is stopped with SIGINT or SIGTERM on cancellation.
The agent names the spec-root `status.md` and `status.yml` as aggregator-only outputs during normal execution.
The agent explains digest-based skipping: when subtask status YAML digests plus index and reloadable config inputs are unchanged, the aggregator avoids rewriting spec-root status files.
The agent differentiates aggregator CLI modes, including `--watch` during execution, `--once` for one-shot rebuilds, and `--validate-only` as a CI gate that avoids spec-root writes.
The agent notes that on each poll iteration the watcher calls `loadConfig` with default `aggregator.pollIntervalMs` of 1500 milliseconds.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent describes background `spec-aggregator --watch`, aggregator-only writes to spec-root `status.md` and `status.yml`, polling `loadConfig` each iteration, and digest-based skip logic.

evals.prompt: Legacy archive `specs/legacy-export/` has subtasks but no `status/` subdirectory. When execute-spec runs there, what do you start or skip?
assertions: When `<specDir>/status/` is missing, the agent logs or narrates the exact legacy banner `status/ directory absent — running legacy spawn path` before treating the tree as executable under the compatibility rules.
On that legacy path the agent states that `spec-aggregator --watch` is not started and `spec-spawn-prefix` is not run, so prompts omit status file pairs from the prefix.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.
fixture_justifications: Pins `specsDir` so the legacy folder resolves under `specs/` deterministically.
Supplies a minimal index with manifest for Step 1 coverage on a tree that deliberately omits `status/`.
Provides the referenced subtask file so manifest row existence checks can succeed.

files.content:
# Legacy export

## Subtask Manifest

| ID | File | Subagent | Dependencies | Phase |
|----|------|----------|-------------|-------|
| 01 | subtask-01-legacy-export-20260116.md | generalPurpose | — | 1 |

Status: Ready for Review

# Subtask 01

## Metadata
- **subagent**: generalPurpose
- **dependencies**: —

## Deliverables Checklist
- [ ] Note migration

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Note recorded

evals.expected_output: The agent calls out the legacy path, cites the absent status directory, and states that aggregator watch and spawn-prefix injection are skipped while logging the legacy message.

evals.prompt: Subtask 03 on Acme payments just finished its implementing subagent. Per Step 4, what verification subagent do you spawn, how do you run it, and who may edit files referenced by a fix list?
assertions: After each implementing subagent completes, the agent spawns a new `zoto-spec-judge` instance that did not author the subtask, preferably as a background subagent.

The agent states the judge writes verdict and checklist reconciliation only inside `<specDir>/status/<subtask>.status.yml` and its paired `.status.md` via YAML-first round-trips, not by touching deliverable sources or the spec index.

When a verdict is Partial or Failed with `extra.judge.fix_list`, the agent plans to re-spawn the same Subagent type from the manifest with that list rather than editing deliverables directly from the executor seat.
After fix work, the agent schedules another fresh judge pass for that subtask.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent mandates a fresh `zoto-spec-judge` verifier in the background, separates implementation from review duties, and routes fixes only through the manifest-listed executor subagent.

evals.prompt: Judge came back Partial on subtask 02 with a non-empty `extra.judge.fix_list`. What `askQuestion` gate do you use next?
assertions: For a Partial adversarial verdict the agent calls `askQuestion` titled to the subtask id with options to re-spawn the assigned subagent, accept as-is, or abort execution.
The agent routes follow-up implementation work to the originally assigned subagent type with the structured fix list verbatim rather than patching code from the orchestrator role.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent surfaces the fix list, updates or narrates Partial status, and presents the three-way `askQuestion` before routing work back to the manifest subagent.
follow_ups: I pick Re-spawn assigned subagent to address fix-list (recommended).

evals.prompt: All subtasks are Done on the index and judging is green. Walk through Step 5 onward: spec Definition of Done ticks, tests, linting, quality audit judge, onStop CLI, and how the hook backstops slips.
assertions: Before claiming completion the agent verifies every manifest row is Done, otherwise it holds another `askQuestion` before advancing.
The agent reads the spec index Definition of Done section and ticks confirmed Markdown checkboxes so `definition_of_done_status` can populate in spec-root `status.yml` for aggregator mirroring.
The agent runs the project’s full automated test suite during final verification rather than only mid-phase targeted tests.
The agent checks linters on all files touched during execution as part of final verification.
The agent spawns another fresh `zoto-spec-judge` for a concise post-execution quality audit of changed areas.

The agent invokes `pnpm exec tsx plugins/zoto-spec-system/scripts/spec-onstop-check.ts --human --repo-root` (with the real repo root) before writing the execution report and treats exit code 2 as blocking until critical inconsistencies are resolved.
The agent notes the Cursor stop hook `hooks/zoto-onstop-check.mjs` runs the same consistency library as a backstop.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent sequences final checks, ticks index DoD boxes with evidence, runs the full suite and lint passes, spawns a concise judge audit, runs the onStop script, and ties the hook to the same library.

evals.prompt: Finish the Acme payments run: write the durable execution report file with UTC start and end times and duration, then ask me to approve marking the spec Completed.
assertions: Before writing the report the agent runs `date -u '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC'` again to capture Completed and computes duration from the stored start instant.
The agent writes `execution-report-acme-payments-20260116.md` (or equivalent dated name) under the spec directory with the markdown sections the skill lists.
The agent ends with `askQuestion` titled Spec Execution Complete offering approve or reject completion options tied to the written report path.
After approval the agent updates the spec index status to Completed and records final execution notes in the index.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent captures end time with the UTC date command, writes `execution-report-[feature]-[yyyymmdd].md`, summarizes verification and tests, and issues the structured completion `askQuestion`.

evals.prompt: Session died mid-run on the Acme payments spec. Re-enter execute-spec, read the index, and tell me exactly how you resume without redoing subtasks already marked Done.
assertions: The agent reads the spec index to see which subtasks are complete and picks the next incomplete subtask from the dependency graph.
The agent explicitly avoids re-executing subtasks already marked Done unless regression checks fail.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent reconstructs progress from the index, finds the next runnable subtask from dependencies, and validates prior deliverables still hold before continuing.

evals.prompt: Subtask 04 failed hard with a severity error in its status pair. What is your user-facing next step under execute-spec error handling?
assertions: The agent does not silently skip the failed subtask or continue dependents without user direction.
The agent uses `askQuestion` to offer structured retry, skip, or abort options consistent with the Error Handling rules.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent updates index status for the failed subtask, halts dependent work, and presents structured recovery choices via `askQuestion`.

evals.prompt: While `spec-aggregator --watch` was running, somebody saved a broken `config.yml` and validation threw ConfigValidationError on reload. What happens to the running watch and what lands in spec-root `status.yml`?
assertions: The agent states the watcher keeps the last valid in-memory configuration when reload validation fails.
The agent states spec-root `status.yml` gains an `events[]` entry with severity error and kind `config_reload_failed` while execution continues under the prior good config.
If `spec-spawn-prefix.ts` exits non-zero, the agent states the executor reuses the last good prefix for the next spawn.

primitive_analysis.summary: Covers the execute-spec skill from configuration and manifest validation through gated execution, parallel phased spawns, status and aggregator behavior (modern vs legacy), mandatory adversarial judging with non-interference and fix-list routing, final onStop and reporting gates, and resume or failure handling.

evals.expected_output: The agent explains prior-config retention, continued watching, and the recorded error event on spec-root status.
